channel.title: NYT > Movies
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: ‘Yes’ Review: From Israel, an Uncomfortable Existential Howl
item.description: Nadav Lapid’s scathing tone and accelerated rhythms lead this movie about a married couple who ingratiate themselves with the country’s power elite.
item.dc:creator: Manohla Dargis

item.title: When a Not-So-Dark Knight and His Sidekick Saved a Wacky Gotham
item.description: Joel Schumacher apologized for “Batman & Robin,” his corny 1997 superhero movie, but thanks to its ice puns and bat nipples, it’s since become an accidental parody worth howling at.
item.dc:creator: Maya Salam

item.title: ‘BTS: The Return’ Review: Back in the Recording Booth
item.description: This documentary follows the K-pop supergroup BTS reuniting after a four-year hiatus as they find their album’s new vision.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Yu
item.media:credit: Roland De Courson/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: BTS performing on March 21 in Gwanghwamun Square in Seoul, as part of their comeback tour.

item.title: ‘They Will Kill You’ Review: The Co-Op From Hell
item.description: Zazie Beetz fights her way through a high-rise of horrors in a splatter-fest that concentrates its creativity in its gore.
item.dc:creator: Ben Kenigsberg

item.title: ‘Alpha’ Review: Body Trouble
item.description: A reckless incident at a party throws a young girl’s life into turmoil in this extraordinary third feature from Julia Ducournau.
item.dc:creator: Jeannette Catsoulis

item.title: ‘A Little Prayer,’ ‘Splitsville’ and More Streaming Gems
item.description: Two of last year’s best indies — a family drama and a no-holds-barred sex comedy — are among the many treasures on your subscription streaming services this month.
item.dc:creator: Jason Bailey

item.title: What the ‘Project Hail Mary’ Directors Learned From a Firing
item.description: Phil Lord and Christopher Miller, best known for animations like the “Spider-Verse” films, took lessons from “Solo: A Star Wars Story,” a project from which they were dismissed.
item.dc:creator: Esther Zuckerman
item.media:credit: Lila Barth for The New York Times
item.media:description: Phil Lord, left, and Christopher Miller. “It was almost contrarian to make a movie that’s so affirming about a topic that is on everyone’s mind, which is this idea that we’re doomed,” Lord said.

item.title: A Master of Animation Is Back, With a ‘Magnificent’ Story
item.description: The first animated feature in 15 years by the acclaimed French filmmaker Sylvain Chomet, the “Triplets of Belleville” creator, is a biopic of another artist who never lost his ability for wonder and curiosity.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Aguilar
item.media:credit: Elliott Verdier for The New York Times
item.media:description: “I’m quite faithful to the child I was,” said Sylvain Chomet, “because I actually became what I wanted to be.”

item.title: FKA twigs Says in Suit That Shia LaBeouf Had Her Sign ‘Illegal’ NDA
item.description: The suit said the nondisclosure agreement that was part of her court settlement with Mr. LaBeouf, her former boyfriend, effectively silenced her. His lawyer disputes that.
item.dc:creator: Melena Ryzik
item.media:credit: Andy Kropa (FKA Twigs) and Lewis Joy/Invision, via Associated Press
item.media:description: The filing is the latest chapter in a long legal battle between the singer FKA twigs, left, and her former boyfriend, the actor Shia LaBeouf.

item.title: ‘She Dances’ Review: Steve Zahn Is a Reluctant Chaperone
item.description: Zahn plays the father of Claire (his real-life daughter, Audrey), awkwardly accompanying her and a friend to a competition in Kentucky.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Kenny

item.title: ‘Our Hero, Balthazar’ Review: Lost Souls and Found Follies
item.description: Opposites attract in this provocative drama about mass violence and two disaffected man-boys.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Kennedy

item.title: ‘A Magnificent Life’ Review: An Era of Artistic Revolutions
item.description: Sylvain Chomet (“The Triplets of Belleville”), directs this animated biopic of the filmmaker Marcel Pagnol, which revels in the sights and sounds of Provence.
item.dc:creator: Beatrice Loayza

item.title: ‘Forbidden Fruits’ Review: Queen Bees of the Food Court
item.description: Among a group of young women at the mall, power becomes toxic in the style of modern classics like “Mean Girls” and “The Craft,” but without the vibrancy.
item.dc:creator: Chris Azzopardi

item.title: ‘Fantasy Life’ Review: Digging Out of the Doldrums
item.description: Matthew Shear’s comedic feature appealingly explores the jitters of a stalled young paralegal who babysits for a frustrated actress.
item.dc:creator: Nicolas Rapold

item.title: In ‘The AI Doc,’ Sam Altman and Dario Amodei Go on the Record
item.description: “The AI Doc: Or How I Became an Apocaloptimist” tries to cover so much that it ends up being more confusing than clarifying, but parts are fascinating.
item.dc:creator: Alissa Wilkinson
item.category: The AI Doc: Or How I Became an Apocaloptimist (Movie)
item.media:description: A scene from “The AI Doc: Or How I Became an Apocaloptimist,” directed by Daniel Roher and Charlie Tyrell.

item.title: Stephen Colbert Is Writing a New ‘Lord of the Rings’ Movie
item.description: In an announcement video with the director Peter Jackson, the late-night host said he was developing a new film based on early chapters of the trilogy.
item.dc:creator: Ali Watkins
item.media:description: Stephen Colbert will focus on writing a new “Lord of the Rings” movie after his talk show ends in May.

item.title: ‘Dog Day Afternoon’ Playwright Briefly Kept Out of Broadway Rehearsals
item.description: A week before opening night, tensions spilled over offstage, with the show’s producing team temporarily prohibiting Stephen Adly Guirgis from entering the theater.
item.dc:creator: Michael Paulson
item.media:description: Ebon Moss-Bachrach, left, and Jon Bernthal are starring in “Dog Day Afternoon,” a stage adaptation of the 1975 film. The show is slated to open March 30 at the August Wilson Theater in Manhattan.

item.title: Comedy’s Most Manipulative Shot … and Its Greatest Weapon
item.description: Audience reactions are a staple of standup specials. But they’re a strange device when you take a closer look.
item.dc:creator: Jason Zinoman

item.title: John Lithgow as Roald Dahl in ‘Giant’: A Study in Monstrosity
item.description: In Mark Rosenblatt’s play, a powerful portrayal of the beloved children’s book author who almost gleefully exposes his bigotry.
item.dc:creator: Helen Shaw
item.media:description: John Lithgow in “Giant” at the Music Box Theater in Manhattan.

item.title: Valerie Perrine, Screen Siren Who Won Critical Acclaim, Dies at 82
item.description: Known early on for skin-baring temptress roles, she later earned rave reviews, a Cannes award and an Oscar nomination for her performance in the Lenny Bruce biopic “Lenny.”
item.dc:creator: Alex Williams
item.media:credit: Chic Donchin/American Broadcasting Companies, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Valerie Perrine in “Malibu,” a 1983 television movie on ABC. “Unlike most sex symbols of the past,” one critic wrote, she “has almost always been singled out by the critics for her acting rather than her anatomy.”

item.title: Who Is Antigone? The 2500-Year-Old Rebel With a Cause.
item.description: “Antigone” gave us the original “bad girl,” but its themes go beyond that. How do adaptations keep making Sophocles’ ideas about democracy and theater new?
item.dc:creator: Helen Shaw

item.title: Chuck Norris: A Life in Pictures
item.description: The actor, known for his skills as a martial artist, often played the warrior onscreen.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Chuck Norris Punched This Article Into the Sun: How an Action Hero Became a Meme
item.description: Norris, best known as the butt-kicking star of action films, became an unwitting if good-natured pioneer of the internet meme.
item.dc:creator: Sopan Deb
item.media:description: Chuck Norris in “Missing in Action” (1984). He once beat the sun in a staring contest, according to an internet meme from the mid-2000s.

item.title: ‘Dhurandhar the Revenge’ Review: A License to Kill, a Lot
item.description: In this ultraviolent rah-rah sequel, Ranveer Singh returns as an undercover Indian agent who’s part political gangster, part antiterrorist killing machine.
item.dc:creator: Nicolas Rapold

item.title: Shigeaki Mori, 88, Dogged Hiroshima Survivor Embraced by Obama, Dies
item.description: He lived through the first atomic bombing in Japan and then spent decades researching the identities of 12 American P.O.W.s killed in the attack.
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Shigeaki Mori with President Barack Obama in 2016. The first American president to visit Hiroshima, Mr. Obama paid tribute to Mr. Mori, “a man who sought out families of Americans killed here, because he believed their loss was equal to his own.”

item.title: Five Action Movies to Stream Now
item.description: This month’s picks include burned-out paramedics, unlikely superheroes and vengeful women.
item.dc:creator: Robert Daniels

item.title: ‘Monte Cristo’ Review: A Tale of Betrayal, on Two Counts
item.description: An adaptation has a twist that doesn’t track, and songs that benefit from an excellent cast, including Norm Lewis, Sierra Boggess and Adam Jacobs.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Vincentelli

item.title: Chuck Norris, Action Star of ‘Walker, Texas Ranger,’ Dies at 86
item.description: He channeled his martial arts skills into heroic roles in films like “The Delta Force” and “Missing in Action” and in the long-running TV series “Walker, Texas Ranger.”
item.dc:creator: Clyde Haberman
item.media:description: Chuck Norris in 1995 as Cordell Walker on “Walker, Texas Ranger.” The series aired on CBS from 1993 to 2001.

item.title: 9 New Movies Our Critics Are Talking About This Week
item.description: Whether you’re a casual moviegoer or an avid buff, our reviewers think these films are worth knowing about.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: Ready or Not 2: Here I Come (Movie)

item.title: Five Free Movies to Stream Now
item.description: Payback is bloody, according to this month’s selections, which include Park Chan-wook’s Vengeance Trilogy.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Yu

item.title: ‘Lumière, le Cinéma!’ From Thierry Frémaux
item.description: Directed by Thierry Frémaux, the Cannes chief, the movie argues that filmmaking is an act of faith in humanity.
item.dc:creator: Alissa Wilkinson
